answer:
\boxed{**Non-existence with objective existence (or "Gegenstand ohne Sein")**}

Alexius Meinong introduced the concept of **"non-existent objects"** (*Gegenstände ohne Sein*) that still possess properties (e.g., "the golden mountain" or "the round square") in his theory of **intentional objects**. This challenges classical logic by acknowledging entities that lack ontological reality but retain meaningful properties. His work influenced later philosophy of mind and metaphysics.
